page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is an arm64-based compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. It includes a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, but does not feature local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core execution, and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with uncached large-block reads reaching 407.39 GB/sec and writes at 196.56 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and gzip compression scoring in the average tier.

The instance exhibits a clear tradeoff between raw compute density and storage availability, as all storage must be network-attached. While it delivers strong text generation speeds for medium and large language models, its text generation speed for smaller models falls into the bottom 25% at 59.94 tokens/sec. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high network throughput, this server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, including high memory bandwidth (407.39 GB/sec read) and strong software compilation capabilities, though single-core metrics remain average. The instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving where network-attached storage is acceptable.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 96 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and 100 Gbps networking for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
Suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and compilation tasks
